paragraphs.body: Is my dog looking like me?

In the article, Dogs look like their owners – it’s a scientific fact, David Robson shares that due to the allure of familiarity and a dog’s personality, dogs tend to look like their owners, which puts our wonders to rest. Based on the experiment by psychologist Joshua Roy from the University of California, San Diego, in a public dog park, we can confirm that pets look like their owners. First, Robson clarifies that we mostly see similarities in pure-bred dogs, but this is also the reason for this. It is stated that the reason is due to the attraction of familiarity. Next, Robson mentions that personality has a significant role in explaining the similarity between humans and dogs because the owner and the dog learn from each other since they live in the same environment. Finally, David Robson reminds us that dogs are man’s best friend and are a better reflection of our true nature.

The term "reflection" is significant because every fact about why dogs look like humans involves physical or behavioral reflection. I can easily confirm this statement because I have experienced it in real life. My siblings and I reflect how my parents and grandparents treat the pets we have had. My family always taught us to respect animals and treat them correctly because they are human beings. Dogs are an essential factor in my family’s values because we don’t only have a dog; we have a pet, which is another family member, as Robson tried to explain in his article. David Robson infers, “Maybe this is all due to the allure of familiarity: a dog may seem more comforting if it resembles the other members of our family, who we know and love.” In simpler words, humans will find comfort in a dog that already resembles someone who provides us that comfort. Because of this inference, I have had many experiences where I wonder why I can identify specific behaviors that remind me of family members. There have been dogs in my life that resemble my family members and me. They inspire me to wonder how they resemble us humans. Slushee, Polo, and Pluto are my family’s pets. To my surprise, each of them connects with certain family members in some peculiar ways where I can identify with whom they belong.

annotations.context_left: dog may seem more comforting if it resembles the other members of our family, who we know

paragraphs.body: My sister went to college in Indiana in a quiet, empty area with little school spirit. Don’t get me wrong, she had a good experience, but it was a significant change. Imagine coming from a place where everything is about being together with family, friends, and your pets to a lonely dorm, quiet campus, and no pets. One day, my mom and I received a call from my sister, and she was with Slushee, a joyful Border Collie mixed with an Australian shepherd. It surprised everyone because my sister mentioned she wanted to adopt a dog, but we didn’t know she was doing it. After all, she felt lonely, but we never thought she would adopt while studying and doing track and field. What I didn’t know was that my sister was going through anxiety and depression. She always told me how much fun she was having because, obviously, as the big sister she is, she was not going to tell me she was battling with anxiety and depression. For this reason, Slushee was signed up as a service dog, and she helped my sister finish her bachelor’s degree. Everything was okay until my sister moved to her first apartment after graduating. Slushee started having symptoms that worried my sister. When she took Slushee to the vet, my sister was shocked when the veterinarian informed her that Slushee was also anxious. They had to prescribe pills to help her with the anxiety. Now my sister jokes around, saying that they are made for each other because they both were taking medication for the same thing and at the same time. At that time, it seemed impossible that Slushee had anxiety, too, just because my sister had it.

annotations.comment: This is still such a funny story :)
annotations.context_right: time. At that time, it seemed impossible that Slushee had anxiety, too, just because my sister had

paragraphs.body: While reading Robson’s article, my mind went straight to her experience. Robson reminds us, “Today, they look like us, act like us, and – unlike other humans – they always reciprocate our feelings.” In other words, they end up feeling how we are feeling. Slushee took my sister’s anxiety away, but she ended up with it. I have always wondered how that happened, but Robson’s article clarified that since your pet lives in the same environment, it learns about your personality the same way you know about it. My family and I had another dog that impacted our lives immensely. I grew up with this dog like we were siblings. I was one year older than he was. Each family member had a special relationship with Polo, but my mom’s relationship with him was the strongest. My mom picked him out of the basket. Polo was the only dark golden out of all his brothers and sisters. That grabbed my mom’s attention and brought him home. She surprised my sister and me, but I don’t remember because I was a toddler. My mom had a lot of stories about Polo when he was a puppy. She even told us that he had fallen from the second floor and had survived. My mom was the only person he respected when he was an adult dog. He followed instructions from everyone, but he greatly respected my mom. Polo stayed beside my mom when we had visitors, like “protecting her” from the visitors. I never understood why they always had a stronger relationship than with any other family member. When Polo was 10, I could find some physical similarities between him and my mom. For example, they had the same hair color. My mom always used to say how she tried to dye her hair a certain way, and it would turn the same color as before again. But now that I am older, I see similarities on an emotional level. They both had very strong personalities and chose who they trusted. I can see why Polo respected my mom so much. He respected my mom the same way everyone in my mom’s life respects her. She is a tough woman and a fantastic human being but doesn’t open up to anyone easily. Polo guarded her because, as a dog, he perceived that she would have her guard up with people. I respect her because she is my mother and because of everything she has been through. In the same way, I know she has worked so hard all her life to be where she is today; Polo, being the intelligent dog he was, probably knew that too. I’m sure he knew he was helping her one way or another. I can’t believe how a dog and a human can learn so much about each other and have a bond with physical and emotional similarities. Polo and my mother are similar because they act strictly and severely until you gain their trust.

annotations.text: Robson reminds us, “Today, they look like us, act like us, and – unlike other humans – they always reciprocate our feelings.” In other
They both had very strong personalities and chose who they trusted.

annotations.context_left: severely until you gain their trust.

paragraphs.body: Currently, I have a dog named Pluto. He isn’t even supposed to be in my house, but I will explain why. My sister rescued Pluto in the summer after her first year of college in 2019. He was such a peculiar-looking dog. He had a big head with a tiny body and giant paws. My family and I thought he would be a tall dog because of the size of his paws. It wasn’t anything like that.

He is a big dog, but he’s short. When Pluto was in his “toddler” phase, he got on my mom’s nerves a lot to the point where she wanted to give him under adoption. I begged her not to; I promised her it was just a phase and that he would grow out of it. I quickly got attached to Pluto because of my love for animals. Now that he is an adult dog, he behaves much better.

Another thing Pluto does, now that he is an adult dog, nobody can annoy him when he is in his dog's house, sleeping or relaxing. He won’t bite you but lets you know he doesn’t want to be bothered. In the same way, I don’t like to be bothered when doing stuff. One last thing we have in common is our respect for my mom. When Pluto knows he did something wrong, he lowers his ears and looks guilty. When I know I have done something wrong, I avoid my mom, too. I am mostly afraid to hear the words “I told you so,” but Pluto and I both learn mostly the harsh ways in some situations.

While thinking about Pluto and discussing it with others, I realized it reflects my behavior. As a young teenager, I got on my mom’s nerves a lot because I did anything I wanted to do. Obviously, she wasn’t going to give me up on adoption, but she also learned how to deal with me and how to manage my behaviors. Now, my mom and I are closer than ever, and I trust her more than before. Even though she is my mother and knows me more than anyone, she had to be patient and understand I didn’t have the same personality as my older sister.

annotations.text: While thinking about Pluto and discussing it with others, I realized it reflects my behavior. As a young teenager, I got on my mom’s nerves a lot because I did anything I wanted to do. Obviously, she wasn’t going to give me up on adoption, but she also learned how to deal with me and how to manage my behaviors.

paragraphs.body: I know that I will never forget Slushee, Polo, or Pluto. These three dogs have impacted my life in a way I would never have imagined a dog could. They showed me the true definition of why dogs are man’s best friend. Slushee went through such a difficult time, but she was still happy seeing my sister happy. Every day, when my sister arrived home from work, she waited for her at the door, and when she saw her, she jumped on her and made her cute little wiggles. Because Polo is important to me, I have always said that my first dog, when I am older, will be a golden retriever. I know that the dog I will have in the future won’t be Polo, but at least it can help me miss him less. Pluto is my reflection. I would have never thought a dog my sister rescued on the side of the street would have such a similar behavior as mine. Dogs are such fascinating creatures, and finding these reflections they tend to show to people closer to me brings me closer to them.

annotations.text: Dogs are such fascinating creatures, and finding these reflections they tend to show to people closer to me brings me closer to them.

paragraphs.body: I find it fascinating how dogs people buy or adopt look and act like their owners. Dogs have different understandings of the world but end up living in similar experiences. In my experience, it is surprising how Slushee resembles my sister, Polo resembles my mom, and without this analysis, I wouldn’t have ever noticed how Pluto resembles me. As it sparked curiosity, I am sure others may have the same thoughts and questions. We can take some time to acknowledge and study our pets to see how they try to resemble our best features. I learned that pets see their owners in their most vulnerable, authentic selves, so depending on how a dog behaves at certain moments helps us understand and get to know someone on a deeper level. It is now fun to see that it is not a coincidence that dogs look like humans; it is scientifically proven! Works Cited David Robson..
